Title: MY WeChatPay
Content:
Last Update : Jan 7, 2026

MO PIC : Kon Nee Looi

Xendit Stakeholders

Department

PIC

PIC Role

Partnership

Musa Sharuddin

Partnership PIC for WeChatPay

Partner PIC

For email communication with WeChat Team, please use email address, operation@payex.io to WeChat Team for email communication.

PIC

PIC Role

Joceli Chee (Ming Ming)

v_mivgmchee@global.tencent.com

David

bchong@global.tencent.com

Eligibility

Entity Type

Eligible

Local Domestic MY Entity Sdn Bhd / Bhd

✅

Local Domestic MY Entity Sole Prop

✅

Local Domestic MY Entity Association

✅

Local Domestic MY Entity LLP / Partnership

✅

Foreign Entity

❌

Local / Foreign Individual

❌

Merchant Requirements

Category

Requirements

Permitted Categories for Transactions of Cross-border Electronic Commerce

  • Trade of goods

  • Airline tickets

  • Hotel accommodation

  • Overseas education

  • Ticket services for tourism

  • International logistics

  • International car rental

  • International conferences

  • Software services (digital entertainment products and Apps download services, excluding top up services for virtual asset, such as top up for online games or membership)

  • Medical services

Permitted Categories for Transactions of Offline QR Code Scanning

  • Tourism shopping

  • Accommodation

  • Catering

  • Transportation

  • Daily life services

  • Tickets services for tourism

  • Leisure and entertainment

  • Communication

  • Medical services

  • Overseas education

  • Training

  • International conference

For more information, refer to: https://pay.weixin.qq.com/index.php/public/wechatpay_en/proper_rule

Forbidden and Restricted Categories

  • Payday loans

  • Stock and securities

  • Mutual Funds

  • Insurance products and services (excluding airline accident insurance, overseas travel accident insurance, overseas car rental accident insurance and other overseas travel accident insurance, freight related insurance, student health insurance for study abroad, ect.)

  • Financial products and services, including trusts or asset management services, or products and services associated with the sale of traveler’s checks, money orders or foreign currency

  • Foreign exchange services or check cashing businesses

  • Peer to peer (P2P) lending services

  • Payment by instalments service

  • Transactions that are associated with purchases of annuities or lottery contracts, lay-away systems, off-shore banking or transactions to finance or refinance debts funded by a credit card

  • Trading or distribution of currency (including USD and other foreign currencies)

  • Pornographic and/or erotic products, pictures, channels, and publications

  • Pornographic and/or erotic services (including but not limited to sex chatting, escort services, and prostitution)

  • Gambling devices and accessories

  • Gambling or betting, including but not limited to lottery tickets, casino gaming chips, off-track betting, memberships on gambling-related internet sites and wagers at races

  • Illegally sold drugs, alcohol, or drug paraphernalia or other products that present a risk to consumer safety

  • Products or services that are marketed or advertised using deceptive or unfair sales practices

  • Ammunition, firearms, or certain firearm parts or accessories, including but not limited to military or police equipment

  • Consumer Credit Reporting Agencies

  • Sales of personal information (e.g. valid or synthetic identity card information)

  • Services or products that infringe on personal privacy (e.g. online activity monitoring)

  • Credit forgery or identity theft services

  • Illegal sale of financial information (e.g. bank accounts, bank cards)

  • Rare wildlife (living things), Rare wildlife products (specimens, etc.)

  • Smuggled goods

  • Human organs

  • Online sale of tobacco products

  • Trading or sale of digital virtual or crypto- currencies (e.g. Bitcoin, Litecoin)

  • Certain weapons or knives regulated under Applicable Law

  • Poisonous or hazardous chemicals or products containing prohibited ingredients

  • Counterfeit or replica goods/products or currency

  • Open-loop prepaid cards

  • Antiques and arts, Antique reproduction shops relating with： i looting or theft, the illicit excavation of archaeological items, smuggling, and the sale of stolen or counterfeit objects and/or ; ii subjective sales price or the transaction price does not match the market price or without a clear pricing mechanism.

  • Precious metals and rare earth minerals

  • Crude oil, Petroleum and Petroleum Products

  • Sale of brand new or second hand ships and aircrafts (Excluding accessories)

  • Pawn service or shops and Salvage Yards.

  • Software or products related to trading of financial products and information

  • Virtual Private Network, Virtual Private Server

  • Cash disbursement services from credit funding sources (e.g. credit card cash out)

  • Rebate or cashback services

  • Products or services that process pop-ups or contain, promote, reference or link to any spyware, malware, virus, back-door, drop dead device or other program installation

  • Real estate service（Real estate development, investment, and sale）

  • Auction sites and services

  • Debt collection services

  • Surrogacy services

  • Hacking services

  • Safe deposit services provided by banks and/or financial institutions

  • Legal services and attorneys

  • Accounting, auditing, and bookkeeping services

  • Consulting services (excluding medical and health consultation, psychological consultation and education consultation)

  • Immigration Service

  • Charitable and social service

  • Crowd funding

  • Illegal or unregistered fund-raising activities

  • Court Costs

  • Deposit and Bail Payments (excluding deposit for shared charger, shared umbrellas, Cybercafe, hotels and rental deposit)

  • Civic, social and fraternal associations

  • Political Organizations endangering national security, including promoting terrorist and extremist organizations, subversion of state power, and disseminating state secrets

  • All religious websites, publications, or accessories (excluding temple souvenirs, tickets)

  • Items that support pyramid or Ponzi scheme, matrix programs or other “get rich quick” schemes or certain multi-level marketing programs

  • Dating and Chat

  • Live e-commerce with reward function

  • Superstition services (e.g. Feng Shui, fortune-telling or tarot divination services)

  • Game account rental platform

  • Cemetery sale

  • NFT (Non-Fungible Token)

  • Sale of raw materials and equipment for drug production, manufacturing, and trafficking of drugs, etc.

  • Fraudulent activities such as click farming, talent agent fraud, false pretenses, etc.

  • Cloud server service

  • Academic fraud, essay ghostwriting, proxy test taking

  • Cheating programs or software that modify game data for the benefit of players

  • Investment and Financial management

  • Course training that induces users to make investments

  • Financial data services

  • Banking or payment products and services (including all types of loans)

  • Business Registration Service

  • Advertising, trademark design, etc.

  • Sale of medicines without applicable registration and/or approval

  • Sale of wine futures

  • Membership fees payable to political parties, association, charitable organization, and other organizations out of the approved scope

  • Products and/or services that are targeted for sale to the institutions (including enterprises, companies and other organizations) (e.g. advertising, enterprise management service, enterprise information technology service, exhibition service, machinery and equipment procurement and/or raw material procurement, etc.)

  • Other goods or services that violate relevant PRC laws and regulations or adversely affect the reputation of Weixin Pay

For more information, refer to: https://pay.weixin.qq.com/index.php/public/wechatpay_en/proper_rule

Restricted Categories Requiring Pre-Approval (not an exhaustive list)

Subject to the Chinese applicable laws, statute, ordinance, regulations, and the approval of Tenpay, Weixin Pay Cross-border Payment Service is restricted for transactions as listed below, please contact our Business Team directly to obtain pre-approval:

  • Single-purpose or other closed-loop prepaid cards and top-ups (including gift cards and other stored value cards)

  • Online Gaming

  • Notary Service

  • Fees payment agency platform

  • Sale of domain name

For more information, refer to: https://pay.weixin.qq.com/index.php/public/wechatpay_en/proper_rule

Other H5 limitation

Terms of Use

To become a WeChat Pay merchant, the merchant has or plans to develop an H5 mall website and belongs to the country, region and industry that has applied for open H5 payment capabilities

H5 payment product capabilities support merchants in most countries, regions, and industries. Merchants can submit relevant materials to apply for activation based on the qualification requirements of different regions and industries (for application process and materials, please see Pre-access Preparation).

Countries and regions where H5 payment capabilities are not available:

  • Indonesia

  • Cambodia

  • Laos

Industries not yet eligible for H5 payment capabilities:

  • Digital Goods

    • Games (MCC=5816)

    • Large Digital Goods Merchant (MCC=5818)

    • Computer programming, data processing and integrated systems design services (MCC=7372)

For more information, refer to: https://pay.weixin.qq.com/doc/global/v3/zh/4012354168

SLA

Entity Origin

SLA

Local Domestic MY Merchants

3 - 5 WD

Onboarding Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Channel Activation

1

Merchant applies via their own Xendit dashboard.

Merchant

MO Process Merchant’s Request

2

MO receive the request in Admin Dashboard under Channel Approvals and Activations

The request will be both for QR and H5 unless otherwise specified.

Merchant/Sales/CS/OIM

3

Check request via Admin Dashboard > Channel Approvals and Activations > choose ewallet, tick on MY_WECHATPAY > click Apply Filter > Click under Action (choose export)

MO

4

In pop up msg Export > Payment Channel: choose My Wechatpay > Status: choose New Submission > Date: to choose accordingly (eg. today) & ensure date range within 14 days > click Submit

Will appear directory URL, to click on the URL (or will also receive email):

5

In Google Drive URL link, to click on excel file with today’s date:


To refer to second tabsheet: Merchant_data :

6

Submit to WeChat Portal:

Log in to https://pay.weixin.qq.com/index.php/public/wechatpay_en using personal credentials.

Login credentials:

  • Email: jessica@640861884

  • Password: 1234@Dcba

a. Navigation

  • Go to Institution > Sub-Merchant > Application > Click + Apply.

b. Basic Information

  • Company Registration Name - put business name as per SSM registered company name (if Sdn. Bhd., to put full with dot)

  • Abbreviated company Registration Name - put business name as per SSM registered company name (if Sdn. Bhd., to put full with dot)

  • Operating Country or Region - select Malaysia

  • Merchant type - select Enterprise

  • Business Category - select category

  • MCC Code - (to download the excel and refer the code there)

  • Registration Certificate Number - put old / new SSM number also can as not mentioned

  • Expiration Date of Registration Certificate - to select Permanent & ticked on Permanent

  • Copy of Registration - to upload merchant SSM certificate [need to screenshot and put as image file only can upload, as format is only: png/jpeg/jpg]

c. Contact Information

  • Contact name - to put merchant PIC name

  • Contact mobile number - to put merchant PIC contact number

  • Email address - to put merchant email address

  • Customer Service Phone number - to put merchant PIC contact number if not available

d. Channel Partner Information

  • Channel Partner No. - no need to update anything, leave empty

Click on Confirm and Next

e. Business Information

  • Business type - to select ONLINE if unsure as we mainly use online QR
    Website: put website with https://

  • (the rest leave empty)

f. Director Information

  • Director name - to put merchant director name

  • Director ID number - to put merchant director IC number

H5 Payment Application

  • Apply for H5 payment authorization - to select YES

  • Sub-Merchant H5 website URL - to put merchant company website (eg. https://www. )

  • Sub-Merchant H5 Payment Authorized Domain (to click Add once) -
    first column: put xendit.co
    second column (added): put website merchant without https://www

Once you click Confirm and Next, a summary overview page will appear. Review all details for accuracy.

Click the Submit button.

Once the success message appears, click Finish. You will be automatically routed back to the Application List page.

7

Back to Admin Dashboard under Channel Approvals and Activations > search specific merchant > click View Details >
Go Action button, click approve (will appear pop up msg, click approve)

Click again on Action button, click send to partner/mark as sent (will appear pop up msg, click same thing again) >

8

MO must continuously monitor the status.

Activation Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Activation flow via AD

1

Once approved, login to WeChatPay website again to view merchant’ s listing to see on approval status > click Approved > will appear Sub-Merchant ID in next tab:

MO

2

Back to Admin Dashboard under Channel Approvals and Activations > search specific merchant > click View Details > scroll down & click pencil to insert Sub-Mechant ID > click Save

click Action > click Activate

Manual Activation

3

In case unable to activate in AD, Xendit MO can also activate channel via Activation Retool. Choose Payment Method: EWALLET, choose WeChatPay (one time payment) > paste MCC (based on website), sub-merchant ID (based on website), submerchant name & address

Example in website:

Example to insert in Retool:

click Activate

4

MO informs Sales/AM/CS/OIM that WeChatPay is activated

Rejection Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

1.

Navigate to Admin Dashboard > Channel Approvals and Activations.

MO

2.

Set filter to Malaysia (MY) and search by BID.

3.

Click the three dots (⋮) and select Reject.

  • Payment by instalments service
  • Transactions that are associated with purchases of annuities or lottery contracts, lay-away systems, off-shore banking or transactions to finance or refinance debts funded by a credit card
  • Trading or distribution of currency (including USD and other foreign currencies)
  • Pornographic and/or erotic products, pictures, channels, and publications
  • Pornographic and/or erotic services (including but not limited to sex chatting, escort services, and prostitution)
  • Gambling devices and accessories
  • Gambling or betting, including but not limited to lottery tickets, casino gaming chips, off-track betting, memberships on gambling-related internet sites and wagers at races
  • Illegally sold drugs, alcohol, or drug paraphernalia or other products that present a risk to consumer safety
  • Products or services that are marketed or advertised using deceptive or unfair sales practices
  • Ammunition, firearms, or certain firearm parts or accessories, including but not limited to military or police equipment
  • Consumer Credit Reporting Agencies
  • Sales of personal information (e.g. valid or synthetic identity card information)
  • Services or products that infringe on personal privacy (e.g. online activity monitoring)
  • Credit forgery or identity theft services
  • Illegal sale of financial information (e.g. bank accounts, bank cards)
  • Rare wildlife (living things), Rare wildlife products (specimens, etc.)
  • Smuggled goods
  • Human organs
  • Online sale of tobacco products
  • Trading or sale of digital virtual or crypto- currencies (e.g. Bitcoin, Litecoin)
  • Certain weapons or knives regulated under Applicable Law
  • Poisonous or hazardous chemicals or products containing prohibited ingredients
  • Counterfeit or replica goods/products or currency
  • Open-loop prepaid cards
  • Antiques and arts, Antique reproduction shops relating with： i looting or theft, the illicit excavation of archaeological items, smuggling, and the sale of stolen or counterfeit objects and/or ; ii subjective sales price or the transaction price does not match the market price or without a clear pricing mechanism.
  • Precious metals and rare earth minerals
  • Crude oil, Petroleum and Petroleum Products
  • Sale of brand new or second hand ships and aircrafts (Excluding accessories)
  • Pawn service or shops and Salvage Yards.
  • Software or products related to trading of financial products and information
  • Virtual Private Network, Virtual Private Server
  • Cash disbursement services from credit funding sources (e.g. credit card cash out)
  • Rebate or cashback services
  • Products or services that process pop-ups or contain, promote, reference or link to any spyware, malware, virus, back-door, drop dead device or other program installation
  • Real estate service（Real estate development, investment, and sale）
  • Auction sites and services
  • Debt collection services
  • Surrogacy services
  • Hacking services
  • Safe deposit services provided by banks and/or financial institutions
  • Legal services and attorneys
  • Accounting, auditing, and bookkeeping services
  • Consulting services (excluding medical and health consultation, psychological consultation and education consultation)
  • Immigration Service
  • Charitable and social service
  • Crowd funding
  • Illegal or unregistered fund-raising activities
  • Court Costs
  • Deposit and Bail Payments (excluding deposit for shared charger, shared umbrellas, Cybercafe, hotels and rental deposit)
  • Civic, social and fraternal associations
  • Political Organizations endangering national security, including promoting terrorist and extremist organizations, subversion of state power, and disseminating state secrets
  • All religious websites, publications, or accessories (excluding temple souvenirs, tickets)
  • Items that support pyramid or Ponzi scheme, matrix programs or other “get rich quick” schemes or certain multi-level marketing programs
  • Dating and Chat
  • Live e-commerce with reward function
  • Superstition services (e.g. Feng Shui, fortune-telling or tarot divination services)
  • Game account rental platform
  • Cemetery sale
  • NFT (Non-Fungible Token)
  • Sale of raw materials and equipment for drug production, manufacturing, and trafficking of drugs, etc.
  • Fraudulent activities such as click farming, talent agent fraud, false pretenses, etc.
  • Cloud server service
  • Academic fraud, essay ghostwriting, proxy test taking
  • Cheating programs or software that modify game data for the benefit of players
  • Investment and Financial management
  • Course training that induces users to make investments
  • Financial data services
  • Banking or payment products and services (including all types of loans)
  • Business Registration Service
  • Advertising, trademark design, etc.
  • Sale of medicines without applicable registration and/or approval
  • Sale of wine futures
  • Membership fees payable to political parties, association, charitable organization, and other organizations out of the approved scope
  • Products and/or services that are targeted for sale to the institutions (including enterprises, companies and other organizations) (e.g. advertising, enterprise management service, enterprise information technology service, exhibition service, machinery and equipment procurement and/or raw material procurement, etc.)
  • Other goods or services that violate relevant PRC laws and regulations or adversely affect the reputation of Weixin Pay

For more information, refer to: https://pay.weixin.qq.com/index.php/public/wechatpay_en/proper_rule

Restricted Categories Requiring Pre-Approval (not an exhaustive list)

Subject to the Chinese applicable laws, statute, ordinance, regulations, and the approval of Tenpay, Weixin Pay Cross-border Payment Service is restricted for transactions as listed below, please contact our Business Team directly to obtain pre-approval:

  • Single-purpose or other closed-loop prepaid cards and top-ups (including gift cards and other stored value cards)
  • Online Gaming
  • Notary Service
  • Fees payment agency platform
  • Sale of domain name

For more information, refer to: https://pay.weixin.qq.com/index.php/public/wechatpay_en/proper_rule

Other H5 limitation

Terms of Use

To become a WeChat Pay merchant, the merchant has or plans to develop an H5 mall website and belongs to the country, region and industry that has applied for open H5 payment capabilities

H5 payment product capabilities support merchants in most countries, regions, and industries. Merchants can submit relevant materials to apply for activation based on the qualification requirements of different regions and industries (for application process and materials, please see Pre-access Preparation).

Countries and regions where H5 payment capabilities are not available:

  • Indonesia
  • Cambodia
  • Laos

Industries not yet eligible for H5 payment capabilities:

  • Digital Goods
    • Games (MCC=5816)
    • Large Digital Goods Merchant (MCC=5818)
    • Computer programming, data processing and integrated systems design services (MCC=7372)

For more information, refer to: https://pay.weixin.qq.com/doc/global/v3/zh/4012354168

SLA

Entity Origin

SLA

Local Domestic MY Merchants

3 - 5 WD

Onboarding Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Channel Activation

1

Merchant applies via their own Xendit dashboard.

Merchant

MO Process Merchant’s Request

2

MO receive the request in Admin Dashboard under Channel Approvals and Activations

The request will be both for QR and H5 unless otherwise specified.

Merchant/Sales/CS/OIM

3

Check request via Admin Dashboard > Channel Approvals and Activations > choose ewallet, tick on MY_WECHATPAY > click Apply Filter > Click under Action (choose export)

MO

4

In pop up msg Export > Payment Channel: choose My Wechatpay > Status: choose New Submission > Date: to choose accordingly (eg. today) & ensure date range within 14 days > click Submit

Will appear directory URL, to click on the URL (or will also receive email):

5

In Google Drive URL link, to click on excel file with today’s date:


To refer to second tabsheet: Merchant_data :

6

Submit to WeChat Portal:

Log in to https://pay.weixin.qq.com/index.php/public/wechatpay_en using personal credentials.

Login credentials:

  • Email: jessica@640861884
  • Password: 1234@Dcba


1. Navigation

  • Go to Institution > Sub-Merchant > Application > Click + Apply.

2. Basic Information

  • Company Registration Name - put business name as per SSM registered company name (if Sdn. Bhd., to put full with dot)
  • Abbreviated company Registration Name - put business name as per SSM registered company name (if Sdn. Bhd., to put full with dot)
  • Operating Country or Region - select Malaysia
  • Merchant type - select Enterprise
  • Business Category - select category
  • MCC Code - (to download the excel and refer the code there)
  • Registration Certificate Number - put old / new SSM number also can as not mentioned
  • Expiration Date of Registration Certificate - to select Permanent & ticked on Permanent
  • Copy of Registration - to upload merchant SSM certificate [need to screenshot and put as image file only can upload, as format is only: png/jpeg/jpg]

3. Contact Information

  • Contact name - to put merchant PIC name
  • Contact mobile number - to put merchant PIC contact number
  • Email address - to put merchant email address
  • Customer Service Phone number - to put merchant PIC contact number if not available

4. Channel Partner Information

  • Channel Partner No. - no need to update anything, leave empty

Click on Confirm and Next

5. Business Information

  • Business type - to select ONLINE if unsure as we mainly use online QR
    Website: put website with https://

(the rest leave empty)

6. Director Information

  • Director name - to put merchant director name
  • Director ID number - to put merchant director IC number

H5 Payment Application

  • Apply for H5 payment authorization - to select YES
  • Sub-Merchant H5 website URL - to put merchant company website (eg. https://www. )
  • Sub-Merchant H5 Payment Authorized Domain (to click Add once) -
    first column: put xendit.co
    second column (added): put website merchant without https://www

Once you click Confirm and Next, a summary overview page will appear. Review all details for accuracy.

Click the Submit button.

Once the success message appears, click Finish. You will be automatically routed back to the Application List page.

7

Back to Admin Dashboard under Channel Approvals and Activations > search specific merchant > click View Details >
Go Action button, click approve (will appear pop up msg, click approve)

Click again on Action button, click send to partner/mark as sent (will appear pop up msg, click same thing again) >

4

MO must continuously monitor the status.

Activation Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

Activation flow via AD

1

Once approved, login to WeChatPay website again to view merchant’ s listing to see on approval status > click Approved > will appear Sub-Merchant ID in next tab:

MO

2

Back to Admin Dashboard under Channel Approvals and Activations > search specific merchant > click View Details > scroll down & click pencil to insert Sub-Mechant ID > click Save

click Action > click Activate

Manual Activation

3

In case unable to activate in AD, Xendit MO can also activate channel via Activation Retool. Choose Payment Method: EWALLET, choose WeChatPay (one time payment) > paste MCC (based on website), sub-merchant ID (based on website), submerchant name & address

Example in website:

Example to insert in Retool:

click Activate

4

MO informs Sales/AM/CS/OIM that WeChatPay is activated

Rejection Flow

Category

No

Steps

Owner / PIC

1.

Navigate to Admin Dashboard > Channel Approvals and Activations.

MO

2.

Set filter to Malaysia (MY) and search by BID.

3.

Click the three dots (⋮) and select Reject.

Merchant Requirements

Category

Requirements

Permitted Categories for Transactions of Cross-border Electronic Commerce

  • Trade of goods
  • Airline tickets
  • Hotel accommodation
  • Overseas education
  • Ticket services for tourism
  • International logistics
  • International car rental
  • International conferences
  • Software services (digital entertainment products and Apps download services, excluding top up services for virtual asset, such as top up for online games or membership)
  • Medical services

Permitted Categories for Transactions of Offline QR Code Scanning

  • Tourism shopping
  • Accommodation
  • Catering
  • Transportation
  • Daily life services
  • Tickets services for tourism
  • Leisure and entertainment
  • Communication
  • Medical services
  • Overseas education
  • Training
  • International conference

For more information, refer to: https://pay.weixin.qq.com/index.php/public/wechatpay_en/proper_rule

Forbidden and Restricted Categories

  • Payday loans
  • Stock and securities
  • Mutual Funds
  • Insurance products and services (excluding airline accident insurance, overseas travel accident insurance, overseas car rental accident insurance and other overseas travel accident insurance, freight related insurance, student health insurance for study abroad, ect.)
  • Financial products and services, including trusts or asset management services, or products and services associated with the sale of traveler’s checks, money orders or foreign currency
  • Foreign exchange services or check cashing businesses
  • Peer to peer (P2P) lending services
  • Payment by instalments service
  • Transactions that are associated with purchases of annuities or lottery contracts, lay-away systems, off-shore banking or transactions to finance or refinance debts funded by a credit card
  • Trading or distribution of currency (including USD and other foreign currencies)

  • Illegally sold drugs, alcohol, or drug paraphernalia or other products that present a risk to consumer safety
  • Products or services that are marketed or advertised using deceptive or unfair sales practices
  • Ammunition, firearms, or certain firearm parts or accessories, including but not limited to military or police equipment
  • Consumer Credit Reporting Agencies
  • Sales of personal information (e.g. valid or synthetic identity card information)
  • Services or products that infringe on personal privacy (e.g. online activity monitoring)

  • Cash disbursement services from credit funding sources (e.g. credit card cash out)
  • Rebate or cashback services

  • Real estate service（Real estate development, investment, and sale）

  • Hacking services
